name: KrillApp.Executor
title: Executors
description: A container node that visually organizes executor nodes such as LogicGate, OutgoingWebHook, Lambda, Calculation, or Compute. Grouping under this container is visual organization only — each executor is invoked through its own meta.sources and invocationTriggers, not by the container. Executors perform actions, calculations, or side effects when a node they observe completes its work.
shortDescription: Container that visually groups executor nodes; each executor is invoked via its own sources.
llmPurpose: Visually organizes executor nodes. Has no execution semantics of its own — each executor wires its own sources and inputs.
llmBehavior: contains executor child nodes for visual organization
no execution semantics — each executor is invoked via its own sources and invocationTriggers
llmOutputs: none — child executors are invoked via their own sources
llmPromptHints: add an action to this trigger
llmCreationHints: Create an Executor node as a container when grouping multiple action types under a trigger or data point. Remember the grouping is visual only — wire each executor's sources to the node it should observe (a dropped child observes its parent by default).

llmConnectionHints.role: organizational container grouping executor implementations; wiring lives on each executor
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Not wired — grouping is visual only; wire each executor's sources (and inputs) directly to the trigger or data node it should observe.

llmExamples: Add actions to run when this trigger fires.
Create an executor group for this data point.
Organize my automation actions under this trigger.
